definition: The Fifth Amendment provision that protects individuals from being tried or punished twice for the same offense. In this case, it is invoked to challenge whether a defendant can receive two separate convictions and punishments under overlapping federal gun statutes for a single fatal shooting.
examples: Barrett argues the Double Jeopardy Clause bars convictions under both § 924(c) and § 924(j) for the same shooting because they constitute the same offense.
